welfare_stack_relationship: Foundational income support; categorical eligibility gateway to other programs
rationale: Largest U.S. nutrition assistance program serving 41.7M participants/month at $99.8B federal spending; 50-state implementation makes it top state database candidate.

welfare_stack_relationship: $16.57B/year block grant to states; funds cash assistance and services; high state variation
rationale: $16.57B/year block grant; state-by-state variation in benefit levels, eligibility rules, and spending priorities makes it a high-value state database candidate.

welfare_stack_relationship: Categorical eligibility trigger for Medicaid and SNAP in most states; state supplements common
rationale: Federal cash floor benefit ($967/month individual in 2025) for ~8 million people; SSI receipt triggers automatic Medicaid eligibility in most states.

program_name: Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI / OASDI Disability)
recipient_types: Disabled workers with qualifying work history
welfare_stack_relationship: Work-history-based insurance; 24-month wait triggers Medicare eligibility
rationale: 7.2 million disabled workers receiving $1581/month average (Dec 2024); Medicare eligibility after 24-month waiting period makes this a critical disability stack node.

program_name: Social Security Old-Age & Survivors Insurance (OASDI — Retirement & Survivors)
welfare_stack_relationship: Foundational retirement income; does not disqualify from SSI/SNAP/housing assistance
rationale: ~60 million retirement/survivor beneficiaries receiving $1.6T/year; foundational income layer for nearly all senior households.

federal_agency: U.S. Department of the Treasury / IRS
recipient_types: Low- to moderate-income workers and families with or without children
welfare_stack_relationship: Largest refundable tax credit; supplements earnings; 30+ states have own EITC add-ons
rationale: ~31 million workers claim ~$64B/year; refundable structure means low-income filers receive cash even with no tax liability; state EITC supplements create database linkage opportunity.

program_name: Child Tax Credit (CTC) including Additional CTC (Refundable)
federal_agency: U.S. Department of the Treasury / IRS
welfare_stack_relationship: Second largest family income support after EITC; refundable portion reaches lowest-income families
rationale: Up to $2000/child ($1700 refundable in 2025); ~90% of families with children claim it; state child tax credits expanding nationally.

program_name: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women Infants and Children (WIC)
welfare_stack_relationship: Upstream health investment; strong co-enrollment with Medicaid and SNAP
rationale: ~6.5 million participants/month; state WIC agencies distribute federally-defined food packages; strong SNAP/Medicaid co-enrollment makes it a key welfare stack connector.

welfare_stack_relationship: Largest public health insurer; SSI/TANF receipt often triggers automatic Medicaid; ACA expansion covers adults ≤138% FPL
rationale: >90 million enrolled; ~$900B/year federal-state spending; state waiver programs and expansion status make it the highest-priority state database candidate in health coverage.

recipient_types: Uninsured children in families above Medicaid limits
welfare_stack_relationship: Fills coverage gap for children between Medicaid and private insurance; enhanced federal match rate
rationale: ~7 million children enrolled; states choose separate CHIP or Medicaid-expansion model creating meaningful state-level database differentiation.

welfare_stack_relationship: Foundational senior/disability health coverage; MSPs and Part D LIS are low-income sub-programs
rationale: ~67 million beneficiaries; $1T+ federal spending; Medicare Savings Programs administered by states provide premium/cost-sharing assistance.

program_name: ACA Premium Tax Credit (PTC) / Cost-Sharing Reductions (CSR)
federal_agency: U.S. Department of the Treasury / HHS
recipient_types: Individuals and families purchasing Marketplace plans (income 100–400%+ FPL)
welfare_stack_relationship: Bridges Medicaid cutoff to full-cost private insurance; IRA expanded eligibility through 2025+
rationale: ~20 million Marketplace enrollees; average subsidy of $536/month (2024); state-based marketplaces create state database variation in eligibility and plan offerings.

subagency: Office of Public and Indian Housing (PIH)
welfare_stack_relationship: Largest federal rental assistance program; ~2000 local PHAs administer waiting lists
rationale: 2.3M+ households assisted; ~2000 local PHAs each maintain waiting lists; PHA-level data is a critical candidate for the state/local pass-through database layer.

subagency: Office of Public and Indian Housing (PIH)
welfare_stack_relationship: Distinct from voucher assistance; HUD funds local housing authorities who own/manage stock
rationale: ~970000 households in federally-owned/managed units; ~3300 housing authorities; separate mechanism from HCV with distinct eligibility rules.

welfare_stack_relationship: Tied to specific units not households; complements HCV; older Section 8 contracts
rationale: ~1.2M assisted multifamily units under long-term HUD contracts; distinct from HCV — assistance stays with property requiring separate tracking.

welfare_stack_relationship: Joint HUD-VA program combining HCV voucher with VA case management and clinical services
rationale: 116000+ vouchers awarded since 2008; available in all 50 states; joint HUD/VA mechanism requires separate indexing from general HCV.

subagency: Office of Community Planning and Development (CPD)
welfare_stack_relationship: Flexible pass-through enabling local housing, infrastructure, and social service investments; ≥70% must benefit LMI persons
rationale: ~$3.3B/year flowing to states and local governments; grantee-level tracking is a state/local pass-through database candidate.

subagency: Office of Community Planning and Development (CPD)
welfare_stack_relationship: Largest federal block grant exclusively for affordable housing; 25-cent locality match required
rationale: ~$1.5B/year; exclusively for affordable housing creation; creates units feeding into long-term rental subsidy and homeownership programs.

subagency: Office of Community Planning and Development (CPD)
welfare_stack_relationship: Homeless services infrastructure complement to HUD Continuum of Care grants
rationale: Funds emergency shelter, street outreach, rapid re-housing, and homelessness prevention; pairs with CoC grants to form the federal homeless assistance architecture.

welfare_stack_relationship: Finances nearly all affordable rental housing development; units feed into HCV and PBRA subsidy layering
rationale: ~$10.5B/year in annual budget authority; largest source of federal affordable housing financing; LIHTC unit locations and rent restrictions are state database candidates.
verification_status: Verified — HUD User LIHTC dataset documentation

welfare_stack_relationship: Service-connected cash benefit; does not reduce SSI/SSDI; triggers other VA programs, some state veterans benefits
rationale: ~5.5 million recipients; $150B+ total VA disability spending; state veterans benefits and property tax exemptions layer on top making state database linking relevant.

recipient_types: Low-income wartime veterans (age 65+ or permanently disabled)
welfare_stack_relationship: Means-tested income support for wartime veterans; Aid and Attendance supplement for in-home care
rationale: Needs-based cash pension for low-income wartime veterans; Aid and Attendance supplement for those requiring in-home care is an often-overlooked benefit.

recipient_types: Veterans, service members, and eligible dependents with 90+ days active service post-9/11
welfare_stack_relationship: Most-used VA education benefit; Yellow Ribbon Program supplements for higher-cost schools
rationale: ~500000 annual users; pays full tuition at public schools + monthly housing allowance (BAH rate) + $1000/year book stipend.

welfare_stack_relationship: Foundation of federal student aid; coordinates with Direct Loans and PLUS Loans
rationale: ~7 million recipients/year; maximum $7395 award (2025-26); needs-based entitlement structure directly affects lowest-income student access to higher education.
verification_status: Verified — Federal Student Aid primary source

program_name: Federal Direct Loan Program (Subsidized & Unsubsidized Stafford Loans)
recipient_types: Eligible undergraduate and graduate students at accredited institutions
welfare_stack_relationship: Subsidized interest is a federal benefit; IDR plans and PSLF forgiveness are downstream welfare stack components
rationale: ~$80B+ in new originations/year; subsidized interest during enrollment represents direct federal transfer; IDR and forgiveness programs are critical downstream stack components.
verification_status: Verified — Federal Student Aid primary source

program_name: Title I Part A — Improving Basic Programs (ESEA/ESSA)
subagency: Office of Elementary and Secondary Education (OESE)
recipient_types: Local educational agencies (LEAs/school districts) with concentrations of low-income students
welfare_stack_relationship: Largest K-12 federal formula grant; funds supplemental academic support for disadvantaged students
rationale: ~$18B/year flowing to ~13000 school districts; school-district-level allocations are public data making this a top state/local pass-through database candidate.
verification_status: Verified — U.S. Department of Education primary source

program_name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
subagency: Administration for Children and Families (ACF), Office of Community Services
recipient_types: Low-income households (priority: elderly, disabled, very young children)
welfare_stack_relationship: Block grant to states for heating/cooling/crisis/weatherization assistance; extreme state-to-state variation
rationale: ~$4B/year; state LIHEAP offices vary significantly in benefit amounts and delivery methods; a high-priority state database candidate due to extreme state-to-state variation.

subagency: Office of State and Community Energy Programs
recipient_types: Low-income homeowners and renters (income ≤200% FPL or receiving SSI/TANF)
welfare_stack_relationship: Permanent energy cost reduction complement to LIHEAP's temporary assistance
rationale: Provides permanent energy efficiency upgrades averaging $2000-$6000/household in federal investment; state WAP agencies maintain waiting lists.

recipient_types: Low-income households (income ≤135% FPL or enrolled in qualifying federal programs)
welfare_stack_relationship: Up to $9.25/month discount on phone/internet; $34.25 on Tribal lands; categorical eligibility based on SNAP/Medicaid/SSI enrollment
rationale: ~7 million subscribers; categorical eligibility based on federal benefit enrollment makes it a welfare stack gateway node; Oregon and Texas administer their own verifier variants.

program_name: National School Lunch Program (NSLP) / School Breakfast Program (SBP)
welfare_stack_relationship: Subsidized/free meals at school; per-meal federal reimbursement; Community Eligibility Provision allows universal free meals at high-poverty schools
rationale: ~30 million children in NSLP; ~15 million in SBP; indexed as one program family (FNS administers jointly); school-level participation data is a state database candidate.

subagency: Administration for Children and Families (ACF) / Office of Head Start
welfare_stack_relationship: Child poverty gateway service; Head Start grantees receive competitive grants and administer locally
rationale: ~800000 children enrolled; competitive federal grants to local agencies create a grantee-level database candidate; comprehensive services make it a unique welfare stack node.

welfare_stack_relationship: CCDBG block grant + mandatory CCES funding; states provide subsidies to families; $12.38B combined federal investment in FY2026; TANF can transfer up to 30% to CCDF
rationale: $12.38B FY2026 combined federal investment; states set copay schedules and provider rates — high-variation state database candidate critical to working-family welfare stack.

recipient_types: Low-income households (via food banks, soup kitchens, food pantries)
welfare_stack_relationship: Supplements SNAP with physical food distribution through food bank network
rationale: USDA purchases 100% American-grown commodities and distributes through states; complements SNAP for households with acute food needs; state distribution network is a database candidate.

program_name: Older Americans Act (OAA) Nutrition Programs (Title III-C)
subagency: Administration for Community Living (ACL) / Administration on Aging (AoA)
recipient_types: Adults 60+ (priority: lowest-income, minority, rural, limited English)
welfare_stack_relationship: Congregate meals at senior centers; home-delivered meals (Meals on Wheels); no income test; targets greatest need
rationale: ~145 million congregate + home-delivered meals/year; no income test; AAA delivery network makes this a candidate for the state/local services database layer.

program_name: Food Distribution Program on Indian Reservations (FDPIR)
recipient_types: Income-eligible American Indian/Alaska Native households on or near reservations
welfare_stack_relationship: Alternative to SNAP for tribal communities; administered by Indian Tribal Organizations
rationale: 110 administering agencies (107 ITOs + 3 state agencies) serving ~85000+ individuals/month; an alternative to SNAP specifically for tribal geographies requiring distinct database treatment.

recipient_types: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own
welfare_stack_relationship: Weekly benefit; funded by FUTA employer tax + state SUTA; ~26 weeks base; federal extended benefits trigger in high unemployment
rationale: ~$30B+ in peak years; wide state variation in benefit levels duration and work requirements; state-level weekly benefit amounts and eligibility rules are a high-priority database candidate.

program_name: WIOA Title I — Adult / Dislocated Worker / Youth Employment & Training
welfare_stack_relationship: Funds workforce development system (American Job Centers); interfaces with UI and SNAP E&T
rationale: $2.9B+ in FY2023 formula funding across Adult/Dislocated Worker/Youth; American Job Center network delivers services in all states making local program data a key database candidate.

subagency: Employment and Training Administration (ETA) / Office of Apprenticeship
welfare_stack_relationship: Paid on-the-job learning + related instruction; DOL or state agency registration and support; WIOA funding can support costs
rationale: ~600000 active apprentices; DOL or state agency registration required; apprenticeship program finder data is publicly available and a database candidate for workforce navigation.

program_name: USDA Farm Commodity Programs (ARC / PLC / Marketing Assistance Loans)
welfare_stack_relationship: Counter-cyclical and price loss coverage payments; marketing assistance loans; Farm Bill authorized; layered with crop insurance
rationale: Multi-billion dollar annual commodity support transfers value to agricultural producers; FSA payment data is publicly available at county level making it a pass-through database candidate.

welfare_stack_relationship: Federally subsidized crop insurance premiums; indemnity payments on loss; complements FSA commodity programs
rationale: Federal government subsidizes ~60% of average crop insurance premium; $17B+ in annual premium subsidies; county-level actuarial data is publicly available.

program_name: USDA Rural Housing Service — Single Family Housing Programs (Sections 502/504/515)
welfare_stack_relationship: Section 502 direct loans; Section 502 guaranteed loans; Section 504 repair loans/grants; rural housing infrastructure complement to HUD programs
rationale: Zero-down financing for rural homebuyers who cannot obtain credit elsewhere; Section 504 grants available to homeowners 62+ for safety repairs; critical for rural welfare stack coverage.
verification_status: Verified — USDA Rural Development primary source

welfare_stack_relationship: Primary SBA loan guarantee enabling credit access for businesses that cannot obtain conventional financing
rationale: SBA's primary loan guarantee; SBA publishes 7(a) loan data by state, county, and industry making it a searchable business database candidate.

recipient_types: Small businesses with tangible net worth <$20M
welfare_stack_relationship: Fixed-rate long-term financing for major fixed assets via Certified Development Company network
rationale: Below-market fixed-rate financing for major fixed assets via CDC network; distinct mechanism from 7(a) requiring separate database treatment.

recipient_types: States (pass-through to vulnerable children, adults, families)
welfare_stack_relationship: Capped entitlement block grant; no match required; flexible for child protective services, foster care for non-IV-E children, and adult protective services
rationale: ~$1.7B/year; served 18 million individuals in FY2023; primary federal funding stream for adult protective services which lacks other dedicated federal sources.

program_name: Title IV-E Foster Care and Adoption Assistance Program
subagency: Administration for Children and Families (ACF) / Children's Bureau
recipient_types: children with special needs being adopted
welfare_stack_relationship: Open-ended federal matching (50–83% reimbursement); triggers automatic Medicaid for eligible children
rationale: Open-ended federal match means states receive unlimited reimbursement for qualifying foster care; IV-E eligibility triggers automatic Medicaid; critical node in child welfare welfare stack.
verification_status: Verified — ACF Children's Bureau primary source

recipient_types: Disaster-affected individuals and households (presidentially declared disasters)
welfare_stack_relationship: Housing repair grants; temporary lodging; other needs assistance; activated by Presidential disaster declaration; does not affect other benefit eligibility
rationale: Triggered by Presidential declaration; provides cash for housing repair/rental and other critical needs; unique activation mechanism requires distinct database treatment from standing benefit programs.

recipient_types: Homeowners, renters, and businesses in NFIP-participating communities
welfare_stack_relationship: Federally backed flood insurance; Write Your Own policies through private insurers; required for federally-backed mortgages in Special Flood Hazard Areas
rationale: ~5 million policies in force; required by federal mortgage programs in flood zones; community-level NFIP participation data is a state/local database candidate.

recipient_types: Homebuyers with limited down payments or credit history
welfare_stack_relationship: Federal mortgage insurance enabling low down payments (3.5% minimum); opens homeownership to households excluded from conventional markets
rationale: ~$1T+ in FHA-insured loan portfolio; 3.5% minimum down payment enables homeownership for households with limited savings; federal guarantee creates value not available in private market.

program_name: Indian Health Service (IHS) — Direct Health Services
recipient_types: American Indian and Alaska Native tribal members
welfare_stack_relationship: Treaty-obligation-based federal health service delivery; tribes can compact services under ISDEAA; distinct from Medicaid/Medicare
rationale: $5.86B FY2026 IHS appropriation; treaty-based health obligation for ~2.6 million eligible AI/AN people; tribal self-governance compacts create a distinct database layer from standard federal health programs.

program_name: Bureau of Indian Affairs (BIA) — General Assistance and Social Services
recipient_types: Eligible American Indians and Alaska Natives on or near reservations
welfare_stack_relationship: General assistance cash; child welfare; adult care; tribal income support when state TANF inaccessible
rationale: BIA General Assistance provides cash to eligible tribal members who are not receiving TANF; separate tribal welfare stack node often overlooked in mainstream federal program databases.

program_name: AmeriCorps (State and National / VISTA / NCCC / Seniors)
welfare_stack_relationship: Living stipends + education awards to members; grants to organizations; VISTA directly targets poverty reduction
rationale: ~200000 annual service members; education awards (~$7395) usable for student loans; nonprofits receiving AmeriCorps grants are welfare-delivery intermediaries worth cataloging.

program_name: Child and Dependent Care Tax Credit (CDCTC)
federal_agency: U.S. Department of the Treasury / IRS
recipient_types: Working households with qualifying dependent care expenses
welfare_stack_relationship: Non-refundable federal credit; many states have refundable versions; tax system complement to CCDF child care subsidies
rationale: Federal CDCTC is non-refundable; many states have refundable versions; the federal/state gap in refundability is a data story central to understanding household-level value differential.

program_name: USDA Conservation Programs (CRP, EQIP, CSP, ACEP)
subagency: Natural Resources Conservation Service (NRCS) / Farm Service Agency (FSA)
welfare_stack_relationship: CRP rental payments for land retirement; cost-share for conservation practices; direct federal transfers to landowners
rationale: CRP rental payments (~$1.8B/year) and EQIP cost-shares represent direct federal transfers to landowners; county-level data is publicly available and relevant to agricultural welfare stack.

subagency: Veterans Benefits Administration (VBA) / Loan Guaranty Service
recipient_types: Eligible veterans, service members, surviving spouses
welfare_stack_relationship: VA guarantees portion of private mortgage; no down payment required; no PMI; major wealth-building benefit
rationale: ~400000+ loans/year; zero-down financing with no private mortgage insurance is a substantial wealth-building advantage; often underrepresented in welfare databases focused on means-tested programs.

recipient_types: Low-income people with HIV who are uninsured/underinsured
welfare_stack_relationship: Formula and competitive grants to states/localities; ADAP medication programs; last-payer health care financing
rationale: ~570000 clients/year; ADAP provides medications to ~200000+ people living with HIV; state-by-state ADAP formularies and eligibility differ substantially making this a state database priority.
